key_insight: CatRetriever learns a shared latent space for slabs and bulk crystals so a generated catalyst surface can be traced back to plausible parent bulk structures.
engineering_relevance: This connects surface generative catalyst design to bulk-dependent screening, helping engineers assess synthesizability, stability, and adsorption performance in one workflow.
method: Contrastive representation learning aligns slab and bulk embeddings.
Treat slab-to-bulk mapping as a retrieval task from a slab query.
Extend retrieval with generative search-space expansion and adsorption-energy analysis for candidate selection.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong retrieval accuracy, with R@1 above 91% and R@3 above 98% on both in-distribution and holdout sets.
watch_item: Retrieval quality depends on the representativeness of the training corpus, so novel chemistries or uncommon surface motifs may still be misranked.
paper_title: CatRetriever: Contrastive Representation Learning for Slab-to-Bulk Retrieval in Generative Catalyst Discovery
